pi_items.source: OpenBCI official documentation (https://docs.openbci.com/Ganglion/GanglionSpecs/) and shop (https://shop.openbci.com/products/ganglion-board). Ganglion is a real 4-channel biosensing board with Bluetooth LE connectivity.

OpenBCI Ganglion official specifications confirm 4-channel configuration. Verified in OpenBCI GUI source code: NCHAN_GANGLION = 4 constant defined in OpenBCI_GUI.pde. GitHub API release info for v5.2.2 confirms Ganglion support.

OpenBCI GUI source code confirms DATASOURCE_SYNTHETIC = 3 constant. OpenBCI documentation describes Synthetic mode as synthetically generated data without hardware. Task description explicitly states 'SYNTHETIC (algorithmic)' mode.

Task description (line 6) explicitly requests screenshot at this path. setup_task.sh (line 14) creates the Screenshots directory at this location. export_result.sh (line 9) checks for file at this exact path.

GitHub API and release page (https://github.com/OpenBCI/OpenBCI_GUI/releases/tag/v5.2.2). Install script downloads: openbcigui_v5.2.2_2023-08-21_16-14-34_linux64.zip. File size: 177,062,645 bytes. Download count: 1191.
OpenBCI GUI source code (OpenBCI_GUI.pde). Also shows DATASOURCE_CYTON = 0, DATASOURCE_PLAYBACK = 2, DATASOURCE_SYNTHETIC = 3, DATASOURCE_STREAMING = 5 for reference.

pi_items.verified_value: Algorithmically generated waveforms, not real hardware or real EEG recordings
pi_items.source: Comparison with other tasks: load_playback_recording uses real PhysioNet EEG data and is tagged 'real-data', while configure_ganglion_4ch_synthetic and configure_16ch_synthetic use Synthetic mode with no real dataset references. Source code confirms synthetic mode generates algorithmic waveforms.

privileged_info_summary: The task configures OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 (released August 21, 2023) to use the Ganglion board—a real 4-channel biosensing board—in Synthetic (algorithmic) data mode. The Ganglion board is a documented OpenBCI product with 4 channels and Bluetooth LE connectivity. When configured in Synthetic mode, the GUI generates algorithmically-generated waveforms rather than data from real hardware or real EEG recordings. The task requires the agent to navigate the System Control Panel, change the board type from the default Cyton (8 channels) to Ganglion (4 channels), ensure Synthetic data mode is selected, start the session, and capture a screenshot showing exactly 4 active channels in the Time Series widget, saving it to /home/ga/Documents/OpenBCI_GUI/Screenshots/ganglion_4ch_display.png.
